meaning: adj.无扎实基础的,无根据的,无稽的
examples: You may feel a bit ungrounded in this new space. It takes time to get used to it.
In a collapse scenario - this is a disaster as one will become frightened, ungrounded and unable to function.
Overvoltages may also BE produced by certain types of asymmetrical fault mainly on systems with ungrounded neutrals.
As big-budget special effects grow ever more ungrounded, the movies that rely on them are coming unmoored.
In response, John LaMattina, previous president of Pfizer research, criticises the book for making what he says are ungrounded assertions and ommissions.
